rows.source_ref: tommor, 'Riding the momentum - NASDAQ100' (BabyPips forum thread, OP post #1), 2024-2025, https://forums.babypips.com/t/riding-the-momentum-nasdaq100/1260721
rows.concept: On D1, breakout of the prior day's high (long) or low (short) in the direction of the 50-EMA, exiting at the next profitable close.
rows.eligibility_reason: Deterministic pure-MQL5 D1 buy/sell-stop with stop-loss at day's low/high on NDX .DWX data; FTMO/DXZ-compatible in principle.
rows.notes: Fully specified: close>50EMA -> buy-stop at day's high, SL at day's low, exit at next profitable close, cancel pending if close<50EMA; mirror for shorts. Author (OP) himself abandoned it (#3): structural negative R:R (stop = full day's range, gains usually < half a day's range) and 40% drawdowns -> Q05 DD gate is the likely killer. Variant discussed (#2/#6) drops the EMA/order-cancel and adds ATR-scaled $-per-point sizing.

rows.source_ref: tommor, 'Riding the momentum - NASDAQ100' (BabyPips forum thread, post #5), 2025, https://forums.babypips.com/t/riding-the-momentum-nasdaq100/1260721
rows.concept: After a higher swing low and a D1 close above the prior swing high, buy the next day's high on a lower-high day (long-only US indices).
rows.eligibility_reason: No stop-loss or exit rule stated and swing-low/high lookback undefined -- too underspecified to mechanize.
rows.notes: tommor's newer replacement (#5) for the breakout method; only the entry setup is sketched, no exit/stop/params.

notes: 15-post thread; single author (tommor) proposes one fully-specified D1 breakout system and later mentions a vague swing replacement. Remaining posts are drawdown-tolerance discussion, no additional rules.
